steps.policy:
**Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and seems urgent.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- [No changes needed]

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

Information '1939.05' communicated in the message:
 'Here's what I found regarding your requests:

1. **Order Modifications:**
   - **Fleece Jacket:** You have a pending order (#W8367380) that includes a fleece jacket. I can remove this item for you.
   - **Skateboard:** You have a pending order (#W1242543) with a skateboard. Unfortunately, the maple, 34-inch, graphic version is currently unavailable. Would you like to choose another variant or proceed with the cancellation of the order?

2. **Grill Purchases:**
   - You have purchased two grills in the past:
     - An electric, medium-sized grill with rotisserie features for $985.66.
     - An electric, portable grill for $953.39.
   - The total price for all the grills you've purchased is $1,939.05.

Please let me know how you would like to proceed with the order modifications or if you would prefer to cancel the order.'

messages.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there! I need some help with a recent order I placed. I accidentally added a fleece jacket and would like to remove it if possible. steps.policy:
**Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and seems urgent.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- [No changes needed]

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence) 

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

steps.policy:
**Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- [No changes needed]

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence) 

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

steps.policy:
**Updated Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "(Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation

steps.policy:
**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- **New Addition**: IF a user requests update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order, upon user confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

The conversation demonstrates effective adherence to the current policy without any notable issues or gaps requiring policy adjustments. Therefore, existing rules remain unchanged.

steps.policy:
**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- **New Addition**: IF a user requests update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order, upon user confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)
- **New Addition**: IF unable to locate a user's account through standard lookup methods (email, name, zip code) → THEN promptly offer to escalate to a human agent for further assistance. (Addition based on trajectory evidence from conversation)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

steps.policy:
**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update.
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update.
- IF a user requests an update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation.
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label.
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order, upon user confirmation.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user.

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation.

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance.
- **New Addition**: IF unable to locate a user's account through standard lookup methods (email, name, zip code) → THEN promptly offer to escalate to a human agent for further assistance.
- **New Addition**: IF user requests a tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN inform the user that tracking numbers aren't available for cancelled orders as they are not shipped. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed] 

End of policy.

steps.policy:
**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update.
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update.
- IF a user requests an update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation.
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label.
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order, upon user confirmation.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user.
- **New Addition**: Always confirm the payment method before finalizing the return or exchange to ensure clarity for the user about where the refund will be processed.

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation.

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF unable to locate a user's account through standard lookup methods (email, name, zip code) → THEN promptly offer to escalate to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF user requests a tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN inform the user that tracking numbers aren't available for cancelled orders as they are not shipped. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

steps.policy:
**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update.
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update.
- IF a user requests an update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation.
- **New Addition**: IF a user indicates that the address used was incorrect, confirm with the user the correct address from their previous orders before proceeding with any updates to their default or pending order addresses.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation.
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation.

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label.
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order, upon user confirmation.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user.
- **New Addition**: Always confirm the payment method before finalizing the return or exchange to ensure clarity for the user about where the refund will be processed.

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes informed selections. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation.

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF unable to locate a user's account through standard lookup methods (email, name, zip code) → THEN promptly offer to escalate to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF user requests a tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN inform the user that tracking numbers aren't available for cancelled orders as they are not shipped. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.

steps.policy:
Here's the updated policy with new rules based on the review of the conversation:

**Agent Policy: Concrete decision procedure for order lookups, modifications, exchanges, returns, pricing checks, and address changes**

Use only rules supported by the example trajectories. Lines marked "Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory" denote behaviors observed once or without proof of mandatory status.

A. Identification and initial user lookup
- [No changes needed]

B. User record & available controls
- [No changes needed]

C. Order inspection and status checks
- [No changes needed]

D. Address changes (account + pending orders)
- Update user details, including email and address, only through customer support representatives. If unable to update directly, escalate to a human agent if the user requests changes to their account information and it seems urgent.
- IF a user requests to change the delivery address for a pending order to an address already available in their user details, directly use the stored address for the update.
- IF a user requests to update the shipping address to a completely new address not present in their user details, ensure to confirm the new address details with the user before proceeding with the update.
- IF a user requests an update of their default address or a pending order address to an address used in a previous order, directly use the previous order address for the update, eliminating the need for user re-confirmation.
- **New Addition**: IF a user indicates that the address used was incorrect, confirm with the user the correct address from their previous orders before proceeding with any updates to their default or pending order addresses.

E. Cancelling pending orders
- Ask the user to confirm the reason for the order cancellation if they request a cancellation. Use provided reasons such as "no longer needed" or "ordered by mistake" to proceed with the tool call for cancellation.
- IF a user mistakenly identifies wrong items for cancellation → THEN verify the correct order by checking item details and confirm with the user before proceeding to cancel. Inform the user of the exact items present before cancellation.
- **New Addition**: IF the user requests a tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN inform the user that tracking numbers aren't available for cancelled orders as they are not shipped. (Observed behavior based on trajectory evidence)

F. Modifying items in pending orders
- Ensure modifications can be executed by confirming item existence and availability before attempting changes. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)
- IF a user requests to remove an item without replacing it, and the tool requires an equal number of items for exchange, inform the user it cannot be removed and offer them options to either replace the item with another or cancel the order completely.
- IF a user requests to remove and replace an item in a pending order with another item, first confirm the availability of the new item and process the replacement through a tool call using the new_item_ids.

G. Variant selection logic for modifications & exchanges
- [No changes needed]

H. Exchanges and returns for delivered items
- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests an exchange → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to exchange,
  - Check availability of requested exchange item and inform the user of options.
  - IF the exchange incurs a price difference, calculate and inform the user of the price difference before proceeding. Confirm the payment method for the price difference and include it in the tool call.
  - **New Addition**: IF the user decides to exchange only part of the initially requested items, confirm the new list of items to exchange before proceeding.

- IF order.status == "delivered" AND user requests a return → THEN:
  - Confirm the exact delivered item_ids to return,
  - IF order.status != "delivered" → THEN inform the user that returns can only be processed for delivered orders and do NOT call return_delivered_order_items.
  - Ask refund destination/payment method (present payment methods from get_user_details and indicate original payment method),
  - Inform the user that refunds for cancellations and returns are processed to the order's original payment method and cannot be changed to a different stored payment method. Offer to escalate to a human agent if the user expresses dissatisfaction or requests assistance with changing refund destinations.
  - IF the user requests the return label to be sent to a different email address than stored, and you cannot update it directly → THEN escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user seems unaware or dissatisfied about the original refund policy AND reiterates wanting a different refund method (e.g., from gift card to credit card) → THEN inform them of the policy again and offer to escalate to a human agent.
  - IF the user cannot remember the email address associated with their account and cannot receive the return label through it → THEN escalate to a human agent to explore alternative methods of sending the return label.
  - IF a user wants to return the complete order instead of partial items to accommodate refund preferences → THEN offer to process a return for all items and refund to the original payment method used for the order upon user confirmation.

I. Mutual exclusivity and one-time limits
- [No changes needed]

J. Confirmation and irreversible operations
- [No changes needed]

K. Payment method handling and price differences
- IF a modify/exchange/return will change order price → THEN explicitly ask which payment method to use, present options from get_user_details, and include chosen payment_method_id in the tool call.
- IF user explicitly requests refund to a specific payment method AND that payment_method_id equals the order's original payment method → THEN use that payment_method_id when calling return_delivered_order_items or processing a cancellation refund.
- IF the user requests a refund destination that is NOT the order's original payment method → THEN inform the user refunds/cancellations/returns must be processed to the original payment method and you cannot change the refund destination. Offer escalation if necessary support is outside automated capabilities.
- IF user requests "use same payment method as before" → THEN infer original payment_method_id from order.payment_history (most recent payment transaction) and use that id unless the user chooses otherwise.
- IF there is a price difference after modifying an order → THEN process any refunds to the original payment method and confirm the final total price with the user.
- **New Addition**: Always confirm the payment method before finalizing the return or exchange to ensure clarity for the user about where the refund will be processed.

L. Price math and calculations
- [No changes needed]

M. Product discovery and counting variants
- Confirm product/variant availability and price before offering options to ensure user makes an informed selection. (Observed Behavior — not proven mandatory)

N. Tool usage order (operational sequence)
- [No changes needed]

O. Availability, alternatives and user choices
- IF a user requests to return only one item out of multiple from a delivered order, inform them of the partial return policy, confirm the exact item for return, and proceed only with the user's confirmation.

P. Handling tool errors, insufficient funds, and escalation
- IF a tool call to execute an exchange/modify fails due to payment (e.g., "Insufficient gift card balance") → THEN inform the user of the failure reason, present available options (add a different payment method, pay the difference, or cancel/escalate), and offer to transfer to a human agent if the user requests.
- IF user asks about refund timing or requests faster processing → THEN state the agent cannot speed up standard processing. If asked for timing, provide any observed example timeline only as Observed Behavior.
- IF a tool call fails due to item unavailability, escalate the issue to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF unable to locate a user's account through standard lookup methods (email, name, zip code) → THEN promptly offer to escalate to a human agent for further assistance.
- IF user requests a tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN inform the user that tracking numbers aren't available for cancelled orders as they are not shipped. (Addition based on trajectory evidence)

Q. Post-action confirmation & closing
- [No changes needed]

R. Evidence flags and constraints
- [No changes needed]

End of policy.
